Aphid.action_message: Use Neem Oil spray or Pyrethrin-based pesticide. Apply every 7 days.
Aphid.damage: Sucks sap from leaves causing yellowing and wilting
Aphid.pesticide: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL — spray 0.3ml per liter of water
Aphid.organic: Neem oil 5ml per liter — spray every 7 days
Aphid.best_time: Early morning or evening — avoid afternoon spray
Aphid.warning: Wear gloves and mask while spraying

Caterpillar.action_message: Apply Chlorpyrifos or Bt spray immediately. Check leaves daily.
Caterpillar.damage: Eats leaves, stems and fruits causing major crop loss
Caterpillar.pesticide: Chlorpyrifos 20% EC — 2ml per liter of water
Caterpillar.organic: Bacillus thuringiensis Bt spray — natural bacteria kills caterpillars
Caterpillar.best_time: Spray when larvae are young — early stage easiest to control
Caterpillar.warning: Check undersides of leaves for egg clusters daily

Mealybug.action_message: Apply Profenofos spray and remove white cottony masses manually.
Mealybug.damage: White cottony mass on stems weakens plant severely
Mealybug.pesticide: Profenofos 50% EC — 2ml per liter of water
Mealybug.organic: Swab with cotton dipped in rubbing alcohol on affected areas
Mealybug.best_time: Spray in cooler hours — early morning or after 5pm
Mealybug.warning: Ants protect mealybugs — control ants too with separate treatment

Thrips.action_message: Use Spinosad spray and blue sticky traps around the crop.
Thrips.damage: Silvery streaks on leaves — causes flower and fruit drop
Thrips.pesticide: Spinosad 45% SC — 0.3ml per liter of water
Thrips.organic: Blue sticky traps plus reflective mulch to repel thrips
Thrips.best_time: Spray twice — 7 days apart for full control
Thrips.warning: Thrips hide inside flowers — spray directly inside blooms

Whitefly.action_message: Use yellow sticky traps and Thiamethoxam spray immediately.
Whitefly.damage: Spreads viral diseases and sucks plant sap causing wilting
Whitefly.pesticide: Thiamethoxam 25% WG — 0.3g per liter of water
Whitefly.organic: Yellow sticky traps plus Neem oil spray every 5 days
Whitefly.warning: Rotate pesticides every spray to prevent resistance

Dragonfly.action_message: No pesticide needed. Dragonflies eat harmful insects naturally. Protect them!
Dragonfly.benefit: Eats mosquitoes, gnats and small harmful flies near crops
Dragonfly.crops_helped: Rice fields and vegetable gardens near water sources
Dragonfly.advice: Maintain small water bodies near farm to attract dragonflies naturally.
Dragonfly.fun_fact: Dragonfly catches 95 percent of everything it hunts — most efficient predator on Earth

Ground_Beetle.action_message: No pesticide needed. Ground beetles protect your soil and roots.
Ground_Beetle.benefit: Lives in soil and eats cutworms, slugs and underground soil pests
Ground_Beetle.crops_helped: All field crops — especially root vegetables like carrot and radish
Ground_Beetle.advice: Avoid deep tillage which destroys ground beetle habitat. Use mulch for shelter.
Ground_Beetle.fun_fact: Ground beetles are active at night — silent protectors of your soil

Honeybee.action_message: No pesticide needed. Bees are essential for your crop yield. Protect them!
Honeybee.benefit: Pollinates flowers — essential for fruit and vegetable production
Honeybee.crops_helped: All flowering crops — cucumber, pumpkin, sunflower, mango
Honeybee.advice: Never spray pesticides when bees are active 8am to 11am. Spray only after 6pm.
Honeybee.fun_fact: 30 percent of all food we eat depends on honeybee pollination

Lacewing.action_message: No pesticide needed. Lacewings eat harmful pest eggs and larvae.
Lacewing.benefit: Larvae eat aphids, mites, whiteflies and small caterpillars
Lacewing.crops_helped: Tomato, Cotton, Maize and all vegetable crops
Lacewing.advice: Plant flowering herbs like coriander near crops to attract lacewings.
Lacewing.fun_fact: A single lacewing larva can eat 200 pest eggs per week

Ladybug.action_message: No pesticide needed. This insect protects your crops. Keep it safe!
Ladybug.benefit: Eats up to 50 aphids per day — natural pest controller
Ladybug.crops_helped: All vegetable crops — especially tomato and brinjal
Ladybug.advice: Do NOT spray pesticides near ladybugs. Plant dill or fennel nearby to attract more.
Ladybug.fun_fact: One ladybug can eat 5000 aphids in its lifetime
